# Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell

Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution. PowerShell is a powerful interactive command-line interface and scripting environment included in the Windows operating system.

## Description

PowerShell can be used to perform a number of actions, including discovery of information and execution of code. Examples include `Start-Process`, `Invoke-Expression`, and encoded commands via `-enc`.

## Detection

Monitor for PowerShell execution, including use of the `-enc` flag. Event ID 4104 (Script Block Logging) captures PowerShell script content. PowerShell 5.0+ transcript logging can also record commands and output.

## Mitigations

- Disable or restrict PowerShell via AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC).
- Enable PowerShell constrained language mode.
- Use anti-malware solutions to detect malicious PowerShell activity.
